summary: Merged fighter decomp PR that split the later half of `src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.c` into ten address-named `ftCo_800C....c` units, promoted `ft_0C31.c` and the new units to Matching in `configure.py`, and filled in capture/downreflect/fighter-state code that had previously been left in the monolithic file. The diff also narrowed `ft_0C31.h`, added per-slice headers, fixed motion-var fields and item signatures needed by the new code, and updated GALE01 splits/symbols. The PR body is empty, but the decomp.dev bot reports 55 new matches and net +2192 matched-code bytes.

key_files.role: Shrank `melee/ft/ft_0C31.c` text range to 0x800C61B0..0x800C703C and added new text/sdata2 ranges for `ftCo_800C703C.c` through `ftCo_800C7CA0.c`; also split sbss so `ft_804D6580` and `ft_804D6588` are separate 4-byte objects.
Changed `ft_0C31.c` from NonMatching to Matching and added ten new Matching fighter objects.
Removed the 0x800C703C..0x800C8170 tail into new units; retained earlier entry/entry-end code and adjusted `fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate` assert/codegen handling.
Deleted prototypes for the moved functions and left only the earlier `ft_0C31` entry-related declarations.
New matching Leadead capture unit, including `ftCo_800C7590`, `ftCo_CaptureLeadead_*`, `ftCo_800C7800`, and item callback plumbing.
New matching Like Like capture/damage unit, including `ftCo_800C78B0`, `ftCo_CaptureLikelike_*`, `ftCo_800C7B0C`, and damage helper code.
New matching DownReflect/wall-bounce and material setup unit, including `ftCo_800C7CA0`, `fn_800C7DC4`, `ftCo_DownReflect_*`, `ftCo_800C8064`, and `ft_800C80A4`.
Added `mv.co.downreflect` and `mv.co.capturelikelike.x4` fields used by the new matching code.
Updated Leadead capture interface to include an ignored float parameter and an inline accessor used by the new `ftCo_800C7590` callsite.
Adjusted local/global labels and corrected sbss/sdata2 object ownership after the split.

what_changed: `ft_0C31.c` was decomposed into separate address-based fighter common objects: `ftCo_800C703C`, `ftCo_800C7070`, `ftCo_800C70D0`, `ftCo_800C7178`, `ftCo_800C7220`, `ftCo_800C739C`, `ftCo_800C7434`, `ftCo_800C7590`, `ftCo_800C78B0`, and `ftCo_800C7CA0`.
`configure.py` now marks `ft_0C31.c` and every new split object as `Object(Matching, ...)` instead of keeping the old monolithic `ft_0C31.c` as NonMatching.

Previously address-only or stubbed common-fighter routines were implemented/matched, including Mario/Luigi/Kirby/Giga Bowser fallback motion helpers, Leadead capture, Like Like capture, Like Like damage application, DownReflect wall reflection, and fighter material setup.

Header dependencies were localized: consumers stopped including the broad `ft_0C31.h` for moved symbols and instead include specific new headers such as `ftCo_800C7434.h`, `ftCo_800C7590.h`, `ftCo_800C78B0.h`, and `ftCo_800C7CA0.h`.
`ftCommon_MotionVars` gained explicit fields for `downreflect` and `capturelikelike.x4`, matching state used by the new code.
`ft_804D6580` moved to `ftCo_800C7CA0.c/.h`; `ft_804D6588` moved to `ft_0C8C.c/.h`; symbols/splits were updated so each is a 4-byte sbss object.
`itleadead` integration changed `it_802EAAEC` from three parameters to four parameters with an ignored `f32`, and the fighter capture code calls it as `it_802EAAEC(..., 0, 0.0f)`.
`ftcpuattack.c` swapped raw `__assert` calls for `HSD_ASSERT(line, NULL)` and added `baselib/debug.h`; the bot later noted a partial `.sdata` match there.
smart_moves: The PR avoided forcing a large mixed-status object to match by splitting `ft_0C31.c` at natural address boundaries and making each new unit independently matchable.
The header cleanup reduced coupling: moved declarations left `ft_0C31.h` and consumers were updated to include only the new per-address header that owns the symbol they call.
The symbols update treated many constants/strings as local `@NNN` labels rather than preserving misleading module-looking globals, which better reflects split-object ownership.

The author documented a codegen-sensitive `fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate`: it intentionally preserves `jobj.h` assert file/line/condition metadata while routing matrix-dirty behavior through the local `ftCo_800C6AFC` path emitted by the target.

The implementation kept awkward but matching branch/codegen constructs where needed, including inline wrapper cascades around `ftCo_800C7070`, `PAD_STACK`, and an explicitly TODO-marked branch-structure workaround in `fn_800C7DC4`.
decomp_lessons: When a PR splits an object, bot-reported broken matches in the old unit can be expected relocation noise if the same functions reappear as new 100% matches in the new units.
Per-object headers are useful after a split: remove moved prototypes from the old module header and update include sites immediately to prevent the old file from remaining a dumping ground.
Correct local/global symbol scope matters for matching data; using local `@NNN` labels for literals can avoid fake global names that no longer belong to a split unit.
Add or refine motion-variable struct fields before matching state-heavy fighter code. Here, `downreflect.x4` and `capturelikelike.x4` made the new DownReflect and Like Like capture code expressible.

Do not overname callbacks when evidence is weak: this PR keeps some callbacks as `fn_800C74CC`, `fn_800C7568`, `fn_800C7AE0`, and `fn_800C7DC4` while naming well-understood motion callbacks like `ftCo_CaptureLeadead_Anim`.

Small ABI or expression-order adjustments may be necessary for item/fighter interaction matches, such as the ignored float added to `it_802EAAEC` and the inline accessor used before `HSD_JObjSetTranslateZ`.
naming_conventions: New split files and headers are named by the first function address in the unit, e.g. `ftCo_800C7CA0.c` / `ftCo_800C7CA0.h`.
Header guards use the address form `GALE01_800C7CA0`, `GALE01_800C7590`, etc.
Prototype comments in new headers use shortened address comments such as `/* 7CA0 */`, `/* 7DC4 */`, and `/* 80A4 */`.
Common fighter functions retain the `ftCo_` prefix; non-common or still-unknown helpers retain `ft_` or `fn_800...` names.
Motion-state callback names follow the existing action callback suffix pattern: `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll`.
Local data/literal symbols in `symbols.txt` are represented as `@NNN` labels when not clearly exported globals.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split one source object into multiple matching objects with exact GALE01 text/sdata2 ranges in `splits.txt`, then list each as `Object(Matching, ...)` in `configure.py`.

`fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate` manually calls `__assert("jobj.h", 916, "jobj")` and `__assert("jobj.h", 917, "translate")` instead of relying on `__FILE__`, preserving target assert metadata while still calling the local matrix-dirty helper.
Repeated `inlineB0`/`inlineB1`/`inlineB2` wrappers around `ftCo_800C7070` appear in several small units, likely to reproduce the target's fallback call/inlining pattern.
`fn_800C7DC4` uses explicit gotos and carries `// @todo fix branch structure`, indicating branch-shape matching took priority over source elegance for the DownReflect wall-position adjustment.
`ftCo_800C7CA0.c` uses `PAD_STACK(8)` and `PAD_STACK(4)` around the wall-reflect implementation.
`itleadead.c` introduced `it_802EAAEC_inline` and a fourth ignored `f32` argument to preserve codegen at the call/translate site.

Two capture files contain small `orderTest(Vec3*)` shims; the slice does not explain their purpose, so treat them only as weak evidence of ordering/codegen experimentation rather than a reusable convention.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews are present in the slice, and the PR body is empty.

decomp.dev bot report for GALE01: matched code 68.47% (+0.06%, +2192 bytes), linked code 40.46% (+0.21%, +8128 bytes), matched data 39.77% (+0.01%, +152 bytes), linked data 33.51% (+0.02%, +184 bytes).
The bot lists 55 new matches, mainly in the new `ftCo_800C...` units, including `fn_800C7DC4`, `ftCo_800C7590`, `ftCo_800C78B0`, `ftCo_800C7B0C`, `ftCo_800C7CA0`, and `ft_800C80A4`.
The bot also lists 36 broken matches in the old `ft_0C31` unit; the named items correspond to moved functions, so the evidence suggests split relocation rather than a human-reported regression.
The bot flags `ftcpuattack` `.sdata` as 84.21% after the assert/string changes, which may be worth rechecking separately.
follow_up_queries: Compare `ft_800C80A4` with `ftmetal` material code; the source has a TODO suggesting a possible shared inline.
Resolve the `fn_800C7DC4` TODO and verify whether the goto-based branch structure can be replaced without breaking the match.
Audit Like Like capture state aliasing: the PR adds `capturelikelike.x4`, but `ftCo_CaptureLikelike_Anim` still accesses the union through `mv.co.mushroom.x4` casts.
Investigate whether the `orderTest(Vec3*)` shims in the capture units are still required after future cleanup.
Recheck the `ftcpuattack` `.sdata` partial regression reported by the bot after the `HSD_ASSERT` conversion.
Rename remaining address-only functions such as `ftCo_800C74F4`, `fn_800C74CC`, and `fn_800C7AE0` only when behavior is confirmed.
